pages.full_text:
through farmlands. Charles keeps noticing sunflower plants because he grew one at school.

     We spent the night at Withers, Le __Long Pres.__** For supper we went to Than to the Au __Lion de Belfort.__

     It was raining, so we all slept in the van with John between Simon + Eve. Eve got clunked once in the night with his cast.
__12__th  We drove of past __Lucerne__ + over the __St. Gottard.__ Snow, old paved stone & cobbled road with pink stripe down the middle. Blinding sunshine on snow. We got out + walked around.
     About 18 miles south of the pass we came to the __St. Gottard campsite**__ It was perched on the side of a hill - we were right next to a big field. A slide led down out of our pitch into  the children's playground where William
pages.notes: Page number not visible at top. Text begins mid-sentence ('through farmlands...') indicating continuation from previous page. 'Withers, Le Long Pres.' may be a campsite name — 'Long Pres' could be 'Long Prés' (French). 'Au Lion de Belfort' is a restaurant/inn name. 'St. Gottard' is the diarist's spelling of St. Gotthard Pass, Switzerland. The '12th' date is underlined. Year cannot be determined from this page alone; context (Lucerne, St. Gotthard, European trip) is consistent with the volume range 1989–2003. 'Belfort' likely refers to the town in Alsace, France, or a restaurant name near there.

pages.full_text:
[hand-drawn sketch of mountain pass road with glued-in photo]

Icy cold, earache. The __St. Gottard__ [Pass?]

+ Peter + John + Charles spent hours playing. There was also a little wooden swimming pool with a cover to keep in the heat at night. W, P + C swam. We shared 5 pizza among the 7 of us + had ice creams for supper.
pages.route_sketch_description: Hand-drawn sketch of a mountain pass road winding through hills/mountains, with a glued-in rectangular photo (appears to show the pass road/landscape). The sketch shows curving road lines, mountain contours, and what appears to be a valley. A caption below reads 'Icy cold, earache. The St. Gottard [Pass?]' with a horizontal line separating it from the main text. The sketch appears to depict the St. Gotthard Pass approach from above.
photo_captions: Icy cold, earache. The St. Gottard [Pass?]
pages.notes: Handwritten page number '10' visible in top-right corner. The page contains a hand-drawn mountain/road sketch with a glued-in photo. The caption 'The St. Gottard [Pass?]' — final word partially cut off at right margin, likely reads 'Pass' or 'Passstrasse'. 'St. Gottard' is the diarist's consistent spelling of St. Gotthard. 'W, P + C swam' likely refers to William, Peter and Charles. 'pizza' appears to be the diarist's spelling of 'pizzas'. Text at top ('+ Peter + John + Charles spent hours') is a continuation from the left page.
